concepts.title: Series and Parallel Combination
concepts.conceptBody:
Series and parallel combination of capacitors

(A) Series Combination 

$\begin{array}{l} {} & \text{V}=\left( {{\text{V}}_{1}}+{{\text{V}}_{2}}+{{\text{V}}_{3}} \right) \\ {} & \frac{\text{Q}}{\left( {{\text{C}}_{\text{eq}}} \right)}=\frac{\text{Q}}{{{\text{C}}_{1}}}+\frac{\text{Q}}{{{\text{C}}_{2}}}+\frac{\text{Q}}{{{\text{C}}_{3}}} \\ {} &  \therefore  \frac{1}{{{\text{C}}_{\text{eq}}}}=\frac{1}{{{\text{C}}_{1}}}+\frac{1}{{{\text{C}}_{2}}}+\frac{1}{{{\text{C}}_{3}}}+\ldots \cdot \frac{1}{{{\text{C}}_{\text{n}}}}  \text{ (Series } \text{ combination) } \end{array}$

(In series combination, charge on each capacitor is constant).

(B) Parallel Combination

$\begin{matrix} \text{Q}={{\text{Q}}_{1}}+{{\text{Q}}_{2}}+{{\text{Q}}_{3}} \\ {{\text{C}}_{\text{eq}}}\text{V}={{\text{C}}_{1}}\text{ V}+{{\text{C}}_{2}}\text{ V}{{\text{C}}_{3}}\text{ V} \\ \therefore {{\text{C}}_{\text{eq}}}={{\text{C}}_{1}}+{{\text{C}}_{2}}+{{\text{C}}_{3}} \\ \end{matrix}$

(In parallel, the voltage across each capacitor is constant).

concepts.title: Wheat Stone Bridge
concepts.conceptBody:
Wheatstone's bridge circuits 

If $\frac{{{\text{C}}_{1}}}{{{\text{C}}_{2}}}=\frac{{{\text{C}}_{3}}}{{{\text{C}}_{4}}}$, bridge is said to be balanced and in that case

${{\text{V}}_{\text{E}}}={{\text{V}}_{\text{D}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{V}}_{\text{E}}}-{{\text{V}}_{\text{D}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{V}}_{\text{ED}}}=0$

i.e., no charge is stored in ${{\text{C}}_{5}}$. Hence, it can be removed from the circuit.
